Details: Greater oxidation of 17a-ethynyl estradiol (EE2) was observed with the addition of H2O2 in comparison to UV alone. In one study, near complete contaminant removal was observed with a H2O2 dose of 5mg/L and less than 500 mJ/cm2. Medium pressure lamps showed slightly more removal in comparison to low pressure lamps. An increase in H2O2 concentration increased the rate of oxidation. Transformation products are unknown.
